title: pouteria euryphylla
intro: Pouteria euryphylla, also known as the Brazilian cherry or mururé, is a tropical tree native to Brazil that belongs to the family Sapotaceae. The plant's bark, seeds, and leaves are used in traditional medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which aid in treating various health issues, including fever, wounds, and digestive problems. The extracts from Pouteria euryphylla have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, cardio-protective, and anti-diabetic properties, suggesting its potential health benefits in preventing chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of Pouteria euryphylla include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or orally to harness its medicinal properties.
